rag_rules: If AGREE reports type mismatches on binary expressions, ensure both sides of '=' are of the same type; resolve unresolved identifiers or remove invalid clauses.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace and fix the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes based on their level of abstraction.
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex when writing AGREE clauses.
Generated AGREE code should not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not part of the model's configured dependencies.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable or output representation.
